answer.content: Eligibility usually depends on age, citizenship status, and residency rules in your region.

answer.title: What to do next
content: Check the minimum voting age where you live

answer.title: Tip
answer.content: Use the Eligibility Checker in this app for a quick, personalized result.

learnMore.content: If you recently moved, some regions require an address update before you can vote at the new location.

answer.content: Registration is the step that creates or updates your voter record (rules vary by region).

answer.title: Simple registration checklist
content: Submit before the deadline and save your reference number

answer.content: Most regions require an accepted identity proof. Some also allow alternate IDs.

answer.title: What to do
content: Check accepted IDs for your region
If allowed, carry an alternate ID as backup

learnMore.title: Polling day tip
learnMore.content: Pack your documents the night before to reduce stress.

answer.content: If your name is missing, you may need to register or file a correction request within the allowed window.

answer.title: Try this
content: Confirm your application status if you recently registered
Submit a correction/registration request before the deadline

answer.content: Polling day is designed to be guided. Staff verify your identity and help you follow the official steps.

answer.title: Quick polling-day flow
content: Follow instructions to cast your vote carefully
Confirm you’ve completed the process before leaving

learnMore.title: Confidence tip
learnMore.content: If you’re unsure at any step, ask polling staff before you finalize.

answer.content: After polling closes, votes are counted under official procedures. Results may be published in stages.

answer.title: What to expect
content: Final results are confirmed after official declaration/certification
Use official sources for final results
